dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 3 responding for cardiac arrest in Richmond.
Hello, I'm Alex, an EMT with the rescue squad. We were dispatched for a reported cardiac arrest.
We found the woman not moving, no pulse in her left side position.
She is unresponsive to all verbal and physical stimuli.
Eyes closed with no spontaneous opening.
No verbal response - no sound, movement, or reaction noted.
No motor response - limbs are floppy and non-reactive.
Airway is compromised by the tongue due to unconsciousness - immediate intervention needed.
We are using head tilt-chin lift for airway opening until further assist.
The airway is patent on assisted opening, but she is not breathing.
BVM is being used with 15 liters per minute oxygen.
No respiratory effort - manually ventilating at one breath every six seconds.
No signs of respiratory effort - no chest rise or movement noted.
Chest remains motionless - no breathing, no expansion.
No pulse at carotid or radial - known cardiac arrest. 16. Primary Assessment; skin appearance: Color; EMT: Warm and dry with normal color, but clothing appears wet and cold in patches.
No pallor noted - skin is warm and no cyanosis identified.
All pulses non-palpable - consistent with known cardiac arrest.
NPA is placed and BVM is being used - no bilateral breath sounds.
Pulse is zero - no cardiac output detected per protocol.
Respiratory rate is zero - apneic.
Blood pressure is 0/0 - no systolic or diastolic pressure.
SpO₂ is zero - expected with apnea and no pulmonary gas exchange.
Blood glucose is 125 mg/dL - no evidence of current hypoglycemia.
EKG shows an initial agonal/idioventricular rhythm - later confirmed as asystole.
Family indicates she was last seen well about an hour prior - sudden decline.
Family is unsure of any known allergies.
Family could not provide medications at the moment.
Past medical history includes seizures, reported by family.

dialogue.micro_intent: S.A.M.P.L.E. -> events leading up to the event
dialogue.utterance: Family says she appeared normal on waking - no recent injury or illness.
No verbalized or observable pain - GCS 3 precludes subjective assessment.
Starting with airway, breathing, and compression - following cardiac arrest protocol 2-7.
Initiating manual chest compressions at 100-120 per minute - first intervention per protocol.
Trying AutoPulse for mechanical compressions - but the strap tore during setup.
Reverting to manual until unit replaced - EMS2 will bring a functioning unit.
4-lead ECG applied - monitoring for any rhythm changes during CPR.
Inline capnography is attached to BVM - assessing airway placement and CO2 output.
Nasopharyngeal airway 28Fr placed - securing the airway until intubation.
Site was prepared with benzoin wipe - intraosseous access started at left humeral head.
Administering Epinephrine 1 mg intraosseously - first dose per cardiac arrest protocol.
Line flushed with normal saline for patency after medication.
Administering Naloxone 2 mg via IO - due to family report of heroin use.
AutoPulse replaced and applied - compressions resumed with fixed unit.
Second dose of Epinephrine - 1 mg administered.
King LT size 4 placed - BVM ventilation via the new airway.
EtCO2 confirms airway placement - waveform remains flat due to asystole.
Thomas tube holder secured over King LT - preventing accidental dislodgement.
Capnography remains flat - asystole confirmed with no CO2.
Third dose of Epinephrine - 1 mg administered intraosseously.
Sodium Bicarbonate 50 mEq intravenously given - for acid management.
Fourth dose of Epinephrine - 1 mg administered with no improvement.
No signs of ROSC or rhythm conversion - continuous asystole noted.
Manual CPR continued - planning for possible termination per guidelines.
Contacting medical control - awaiting decision for resuscitation stop.
Glasgow Coma Scale 3 - Eyes 1, Verbal 1, Motor 1 - fully unresponsive.
No reversal in hypoxia despite airway and oxygen efforts.
N95 mask, eye protection, and gloves used before patient contact - per PPE protocol.
Intramuscular access not attempted - IO is primary access established.
Still a non-reaction - pulse 0, respirations 0, and asystole without improvement.
Replaced faulty AutoPulse with second unit - results unchanged.
No pain indicators - patient unconscious with no signs of discomfort.
Family unable to clarify medication or allergies - recounted story confirmed.
I-Gel used for airway support - placement confirmed with observations.
No movement - patient remains in place and declared deceased.
IO secure with cravat tied around limb to pant belt loop for stability.
Time of death confirmed at 2251 - documented and completed per protocol.
Transport not pursued - death occurred on-location.
Informing the family of resuscitation stop - process finalized.
Capnography unit tested - waveform remains flat per current status.
IO site remains patent - catheter secured and tightly functioning.
Ambulance fan operational - environment maintained according to protocol.
Entering critical inbound into ePCR - following online medical control directives.
